key_insight: MOSAIC makes continual learning work better when new gait sensor modalities arrive over time by stabilizing new modality representations, separating sensor-specific statistics, and recovering plasticity without catastrophic forgetting.
engineering_relevance: This is relevant for scalable clinical gait-monitoring systems that must adapt to new devices, protocols, or sites while preserving earlier Parkinson’s disease assessment knowledge.
method: Modality-Specific Warm-Up to reduce the Toxic Teacher effect before distillation.
Statistics-decoupled MSBN to isolate modality statistics while sharing semantic features.
Curriculum-guided repulsive objective to preserve old knowledge and recover modality-specific capacity.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports experiments on three multimodal Parkinson’s gait datasets, with improved final performance and reduced forgetting.
watch_item: The approach adds continual-learning complexity and may require careful tuning to avoid instability when the new modality is scarce or highly shifted.
paper_title: MOSAIC: Modality-Specific Adaptation for Incremental Continual Learning in Parkinson's Disease Gait Assessment
